message:
Add deterministic precedence-based conflict resolution to anti-entropy reconciler

Replace the presence-only delta computation with a three-way precedence
comparison (epoch, recorded_at_ms, origin_node_id) that deterministically
resolves conflicting records without operator intervention. Fork detection
now only fires on true irresolvable ties where precedence tuples match
but content differs.

Key changes:
- TrustRecord gains recorded_at_ms and origin_node_id fields
- New precedence_cmp() method: epoch > timestamp > node_id lexicographic
- New ConflictResolution enum: Identical, KeepLocal, TakeRemote, Fork
- compute_delta() returns records where remote has higher precedence
- detect_fork() restricted to true irresolvable ties
- reconcile() handles TakeRemote (replacement) and Fork (error)
- Record digest includes all identity fields for collision resistance

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
